displayTitle: Bending the Beam: One Mode, One Answer

beats.narration: Weld testing runs on angled beams. Cross a boundary at an angle and sound bends: refraction. Snell's law does the bookkeeping.

payload.title: BENDING THE BEAM
payload.sub: Snell's law and the angled sound beam.

beats.narration: But the boundary also splits the beam: part converts to slower shear at a shallower angle. Two beams in the part at once.

payload.heading: An angled boundary splits the beam
payload.note: TWO MODES · TWO VELOCITIES · TWO ANGLES

beats.narration: One reflector can paint two indications, calibration set for one velocity misplaces the other, and back-wall conversions breed ghosts. Suspect a converted mode first.

chips.t: Ghosts born at the back wall

beats.narration: More tilt cures it. On an acrylic wedge: below the first critical angle, two modes. In the window, shear only. Past the second, Rayleigh waves.
Your forty-five, sixty, and seventy degree wedges are cut with incident angles inside that window. One mode, one velocity, one answer.
